arts.ttl: Serie A line-ups: Bologna-Parma and Lazio-Empoli
arts.sum: Check the official line-ups for this afternoon’s matches, as Lazio take on unbeaten Empoli and...

Bologna have won their last two games against Parma in Serie A (both in the 2020/21 season), and have never achieved three wins in a row against the Gialloblù in the top-flight. Bologna are unbeaten in their last nine games (W4 D5) against Parma in Serie A, already a record streak for the Rossoblù against the Ducali in the tournament; the last win for the Gialloblù in this Emilian derby in the top-flight dates to December 2012 (2-1 comeback at the Dall`Ara, with goals from Jaime Valdés and Nicola Sansone, after the Rossoblù had taken the lead via Frederik Sörensen).

arts.ttl: Pecchia: “Bologna are a very aggressive team, similar to Fiorentina in recent years”
arts.sum: On the eve of the Bologna-Parma match, Parma manager Fabio Pecchia shared his insights on the upcoming opponents during a press conference. Pecchia highlighted Bologna’s key characteristics, stating, “They are a very flexible team with many rotations, lots of players pushing forward, and very aggressive – similar to Fiorentina in recent years. They are a team capable of executing the offensive phase well.” When asked about strategies to contain Bologna’s midfield maestro Freuler, Pecchia emphasized the importance of focusing on the team as a whole rather than individuals. arts.ttl: Serie A | Bologna v Parma: Match Preview
arts.sum: As Serie A action resumes this weekend, Bologna welcomes newly-promoted Parma to Stadio Renato Dall’Ara for an intriguing Sunday afternoon clash. Both teams enter the match looking to bounce back from recent setbacks and climb the league table. Bologna, fresh off a midweek Champions League defeat at Liverpool, sits 13th in Serie A with just one win from their opening six matches. The Rossoblu have drawn all three of their home games so far this season by identical 1-1 scorelines. arts.ttl: Beukema shines for Bologna, attracting Premier League Interest and national team call-up
arts.sum: Bologna’s Dutch center-back Sam Beukema continues to impress with his performances, most recently in a convincing display against Liverpool at Anfield. Despite conceding goals to McAllister and Salah, Beukema’s performance caught the eye of Premier League scouts in attendance. La Gazzetta dello Sport report how the 25-year-old defender, who joined Bologna two summers ago from AZ Alkmaar for €7.5 million, has developed significantly under coach Thiago Motta. His growth has not gone unnoticed, with several European clubs already monitoring his progress since last season.
